title: WebView OAuth login flow for Claude
description: Replace manual OAuth token pasting with in-app WebView login for Claude. Current problem: OAuth tokens rotate every few hours. Proposed: WebView login to claude.ai captures OAuth token, or scrapes usage page directly via injected JS. WebView approach avoids undocumented API concerns. Same pattern could work for Codex via chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage.

title: Firmware OTA updates over WiFi
description: Add ArduinoOTA or HTTP OTA to the M5 firmware so it can be updated over WiFi without re-plugging USB. Requires adding WiFi credentials to the firmware (could be configured via BLE from the phone app).

title: bd sync commit fails: fork protection excludes .beads/issues.jsonl in own repo
description: bd 0.42.0 fork-protection + hook friction — RESOLVED IN PRACTICE, kept open for the upstream bug. Current working convention (2026-07-17, learned over 3 agent escalations): (1) .beads/issues.jsonl is git-TRACKED since commit d0172ed, which neutralizes the .git/info/exclude fork-protection (excludes only affect untracked files). (2) bd's pre-commit hook auto-flushes the FULL current DB snapshot into issues.jsonl on every commit — accept the resulting one-file tracker diffs in feature commits; NEVER hand-stage .beads, NEVER --no-verify, NEVER uninstall hooks, NEVER history-rewrite to purge tracker lines. (3) If pre-push blocks with 'stale JSONL', make one capstone commit (git commit --allow-empty lets the hook flush) instead of bypassing. (4) Plain 'bd sync' commit step may still error; use --flush-only + normal git add (no -f needed now). Upstream question remains: why does bd think tedks/m5 is a fork.

title: Auto-refresh quotas on a timer
description: Use Android WorkManager to periodically fetch quotas and push to M5/Wear OS in the background. Should respect Claude OAuth rate limits (~180s minimum interval). Currently requires manual tap of Refresh button.

title: Claude JS fallback grabs first percentage on page — silent misparse risk
description: ClaudeScraper JS_EXTRACT falls back to the first %-pattern in body text as fiveHourPct, which can silently pick a wrong number if the page changes. Consider requiring labeled context or returning unparsed.
close_reason: Deleted the first-percentage-anywhere fallback in ClaudeScraper.JS_EXTRACT; Claude values now come only from label-anchored modal text (Current session / Weekly limits). Fixed in PR #11.

title: Merged snapshot appends retained-stale quotas at the end — UI/BLE ordering churn
description: mergedWith does results + retainedStale, so carried-over quotas reorder to the bottom of the UI and the tail of the BLE payload (firmware caps at 6 quotas). Cosmetic; consider stable ordering by service/name. Council nit N4 from the 2026-07-17 stack review.

title: Fix framework0 USB enumeration for M5
description:
The M5StickC Plus fails to enumerate on framework0 (USB error -110 timeouts on device descriptor reads). Works fine on tower0. Tried:
- Different cables (including included A-to-C cable)
- USB autosuspend disable
- Different ports

Likely a Realtek USB hub issue in the Framework expansion card system. Would be nice to flash directly from the dev machine instead of going through tower0.

title: hasSession() treats any cookie as login — Cloudflare cookies count
description: UsageScraper.hasSession returns true if ANY cookie exists for the origin (__cf_bm etc.), so Settings shows 'Logged in' after real session expiry and scrapes burn 45s before failing. Consider persisting last scrape outcome per service to drive UI status.

title: QuotaFetcher retains Activity context in ViewModel — Activity leak
description: QuotaViewModel.setActivityContext stores Activity-context-backed QuotaFetcher in a field that outlives the Activity (rotation leak). Activity context is empirically required for WebView rendering (046ec8c) so use a lifecycle-safe holder, not a plain field. fix/lifecycle-refresh.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #8 — WeakReference context provider (+ destroyed-Activity skip in PR #9's nit commit); pending merge.

title: Fragile evaluateJavascript result unescaping duplicated in scrapers
description: Chained .replace() unescaping of the JSON-encoded JS string is order-sensitive and wrong for edge cases; duplicated in ClaudeScraper+CodexScraper. Decode with JSONTokener in UsageScraper instead. fix/scraper-robustness.
close_reason: Implemented in fix/scraper-robustness (PR #10, draft): decodeJsResult() via JSONTokener, unit tested in DecodeJsResultTest.

comments.author: tedks
comments.text: Fixed in fix/scraper-robustness: UsageScraper.decodeJsResult() uses org.json.JSONTokener instead of chained .replace(). Unit tests in DecodeJsResultTest cover embedded quotes, real newline vs literal backslash-n, unicode escapes, null/empty/malformed input. PR pending (draft).

title: Scraper cookies never flushed — session rotation lost on process death
description: CookieManager.flush() only runs in the login WebView (MainActivity onPageFinished). claude.ai/chatgpt.com rotate session tokens during scrapes; rotated cookies stay in RAM and die with the process, randomly logging out one service. Fix: flush after every scrape in UsageScraper. Also clearSession() is dead code using setCookie(url, "") which is a no-op. Fixed by PR stack: fix/cookie-persistence.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #6 (fix/cookie-persistence) — flush after every scrape; pending merge.

title: Release signing for APK
description: Set up a release signing keystore for the APK so sideloading does not show unknown developer warnings. Generate a keystore, configure signing in build.gradle.kts, add the key to CI secrets for release builds.

title: Codex 5h block not parsing on device (fiveHourRemaining=-1, weekly OK)
description: During PR1 device test (2026-07-17), CodexScraper parsed weeklyRemaining=99 but fiveHourRemaining=-1 — the '5 hour usage limit' regex found no match. Either the page section renders later than the weekly one (poll-until-ready in fix/scraper-robustness may fix) or the page wording changed. Verify after PR5 lands.
close_reason: Root cause was not a render race but a phantom section: this account's Codex page has no 5-hour block at all. Removed the 5h regex/field/quota; parse only the labeled Weekly usage limit. Fixed in PR #11.

comments.author: tedks
comments.text: Verified on-device after implementing fix/scraper-robustness's poll-until-settled. NOT fixed by the timing change: across 3 scrape cycles, fiveHourRemaining stayed -1 every time while weeklyRemaining consistently parsed (99, 99, 98). Confirmed via temporary debug logging that the full page text has no '5 hour usage limit' section at all for this account right now — so it's not a rendering race (poll-until-settled rules that out; the page genuinely stabilizes without ever showing that text). Root-caused as a JS_EXTRACT/page-structure mismatch, tracked separately as m5-iia (out of scope for fix/scraper-robustness, which was decode+timing only). Leaving this open, downgrading my expectation that PR5 alone resolves it — see m5-iia for the real fix.

title: Claude/Codex JS_EXTRACT selectors don't match current page — real quota data still doesn't parse under fix/scraper-robustness
description:
Verified on-device (2026-07-17, real accounts) after implementing fix/scraper-robustness (m5-ca1/m5-om5/m5-hf9). Poll-until-settled works correctly — it reliably waits for the page to stop changing — but the underlying JS_EXTRACT text/regex in both scrapers still doesn't match the current page structure, independent of timing:

Codex (CodexScraper.JS_EXTRACT): across 3 separate scrape cycles, weeklyRemaining consistently parsed correctly (99, 99, 98) but fiveHourRemaining stayed -1. Full page text (up to 1000 chars, confirmed via temporary debug logging) shows NO '5 hour usage limit' section at all on this account's chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/settings/usage page — only 'Balance', 'Weekly usage limit', and per-model breakdowns ('GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark 100% remaining'). The regex /5\s*hour\s*usage\s*limit\.../ has nothing to match against; this is not the m5-hf9 rendering-race hypothesis (poll-until-settled rules that out), it's that the page apparently no longer shows a 5-hour block in this text form for this account. Needs a fresh look at the actual DOM/page (maybe behind a tab, or removed from the UI) rather than a timing fix.

Claude (ClaudeScraper.JS_EXTRACT): consistently returns fiveHourPct=2 (then 3 on a later run) with sevenDayPct=-1, and 'bars':[] (no aria progressbar elements found) every time. The percentages picked up ('2%','14%','10%','0%') are the pctMatches fallback grabbing the FIRST percentage found in document.body.innerText — but the visible text at settle time is the collapsed sidebar/chat list (page redirects client-side from /settings/usage to /new#settings/usage, i.e. a modal route), not an actual usage panel. This means ClaudeScraper is likely reporting a bogus 'Claude 5h' number to the user rather than failing loudly — worse than Codex's honest omission. Needs investigation into what /new#settings/usage actually renders and whether the real quota UI needs a selector scoped to the modal/dialog content instead of document.body.innerText.
close_reason: Both scrapers now extract only labeled usage data and fail honestly otherwise. Device-verified in PR #11 (pending stack merge).

comments.author: tedks
comments.text:
Fixed in PR #11 (fix/extraction-selectors), device-verified 2026-07-17 across 2 clean cycles.

DOM findings (mobile Pixel 8 Pro UA WebView, real sessions):
- Claude: /settings/usage redirects to /new#settings/usage; usage renders as a [role=dialog] modal within ~3s (NOT sidebar — the earlier junk reads were extraction running against body.innerText instead of the modal). Modal: Current session (5h window) = N% used; Weekly limits -> All models = N% used, plus a per-model weekly bar and Usage credits.
- Codex: page has Balance / Weekly usage limit (N% remaining) / per-model breakdowns. Confirmed NO 5-hour section exists (phantom regex).

Fix: extract ONLY label-anchored values (Claude: Current session -> 5h, Weekly limits/All models -> wk; Codex: Weekly usage limit -> wk). Deleted Claude first-percentage-anywhere fallback (m5-39u). Dropped Codex phantom 5h field/quota (m5-hf9). Honest Error + LKG retention when the usage UI is absent. Verified values: Claude 5h=8%, wk=15%; Codex wk=2% used (98% remaining), stable across both cycles.

title: Transient scrape failure wipes last-known-good quotas from UI/BLE
description: QuotaViewModel wholesale-replaces _quotas with each snapshot. If Codex times out, its quota vanishes from the phone UI and the M5 payload until the next success — user sees 'only one service picked up'. Fix: per-service merge retaining previous Success results with staleness. fix/last-known-good.
notes: PR #7 (fix/last-known-good) opened, based on fix/cookie-persistence. Awaiting review/merge.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #7 (fix/last-known-good) — (service,name)-keyed retention with active-reporting policy + tests; pending merge.

title: Test Wear OS companion on actual watch
description:
The Wear OS module compiles and the phone app syncs data via Data Layer, but it hasn't been tested on an actual Wear OS device. Need to:
- Verify WearableListenerService receives data
- Test the ScalingLazyColumn quota display
- Check if the watch needs to be on the same Google account
- Test with real quota data

title: Verify background scrape with Activity fully destroyed (pure app-context WebView)
description: PR4's background-tick test passed with the stopped-but-alive Activity context (HOME doesn't destroy). The Application-context fallback path (Activity destroyed, e.g. task swiped from recents) is unverified — commit 046ec8c says app-context WebViews fail to render on some devices. During full-stack verification: swipe the task away on the physical phone, wait a 5-min service tick, check logcat for scrape success. If it fails, next options are documented in PR #9 discussion (not overlay hacks).
close_reason: Verified on device 2026-07-17: with always_finish_activities=1 (Activity destroyed after HOME), the 5-min FGS tick scraped both services successfully (Claude 5h 8→9%, wk 15%; Codex wk 98% remaining). Renderer-priority policy suffices; app-context/destroyed-activity WebView renders fine on this Pixel.

title: Fixed 4s/6s inject delay — poll until extraction JS returns data
description: Fixed inject delay is both slow and flaky for SPAs. Poll the extraction JS every ~500ms after onPageFinished until it yields valid data or TIMEOUT_MS. fix/scraper-robustness.
close_reason: Implemented in fix/scraper-robustness (PR #10, draft): poll-until-settled every 500ms, replacing fixed 4s/6s inject delay. Device-verified latency and correctness; m5-hf9's specific fiveHourRemaining case turned out to be a separate page-structure issue, tracked as m5-iia.

comments.author: tedks
comments.text: Fixed in fix/scraper-robustness: UsageScraper.scrape() now polls the extraction JS every 500ms after onPageFinished, completing when the decoded result is valid (caller predicate) AND identical to the previous poll (settled). Removed INJECT_DELAY_MS/injectDelayMs. On-device: Claude scrape ~3.5-4.5s wall (was fixed 6s wait alone), Codex ~3-4s (was fixed 4s wait alone) -- and now correctly waits out redirects/progressive rendering instead of grabbing a fixed snapshot. PR pending (draft).

title: Auto-refresh stops when app unfocused; no background refresh mechanism
description: Auto-refresh is a viewModelScope while(true)/delay loop: frozen by the Android 12+ cached-app freezer when backgrounded, killed with the process, plus background WebView renderers are paused so scrapes time out (45s) and produce Errors. Fix across fix/lifecycle-refresh (lifecycle gating) + feat/foreground-service (connectedDevice foreground service owning refresh+BLE, renderer priority policy).
close_reason: Fixed across PR #8 (lifecycle) + PR #9 (connectedDevice FGS); background tick device-verified incl. Activity-destroyed case; pending merge.

title: Scrapes run sequentially — consider parallel Claude+Codex scrape
description: fetchAll runs Claude then Codex serially (~10-50s each worst case). Two WebViews can scrape concurrently. Follow-up after the reliability stack lands.

title: Re-add tolerant Codex 5h parsing — OpenAI's removal of the 5-hour limit is temporary
description: PR #11 deleted Codex 5h extraction because the usage page had no such section. Web research (2026-07-17): OpenAI temporarily removed the 5-hour limit for Plus/Pro/Business on 2026-07-13, so the section will likely return. Re-add a label-anchored '5 hour usage limit ... N% remaining' parse that emits 'Codex 5h' when present and silently skips when absent (no error, no unlabeled fallback, settle predicate unchanged — weekly presence still gates settling). Refs: help.openai.com/en/articles/20001106-codex-rate-card, bleepingcomputer.com/news/artificial-intelligence/openai-temporarily-relaxes-gpt-56-sol-usage-limits/

title: Handle Claude OAuth token refresh
description:
The OAuth access token from ~/.claude/.credentials.json expires (expiresAt field). The app should detect expiry and either:
- Prompt the user to paste a new token
- Use the refresh token to get a new access token (risky — can conflict with Claude Code's own refresh cycle)
- Show a clear 'token expired' error

Currently the token just silently fails when expired.

title: Explore Codex/ChatGPT Pro usage scraping
description:
ChatGPT Pro subscription usage is not exposed via any public API. The only surface is the web dashboard at chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage. Potential approaches:
- WebView loads the usage page, JS injection scrapes the DOM
- Intercept internal API calls the page makes via WebViewClient
- Enterprise analytics API exists but is gated to Enterprise customers

Low priority — may never be possible for individual Pro subscribers.

title: M5 second screen (Button A scroll) with Fable/Spark per-model usage
description: Mirror of github.com/tedks/m5/issues/12 — firmware screen paging via Button A (interaction redesign needed: A currently toggles screen), companion scraping of per-model weekly rows from the claude.ai usage modal (label-anchored only), BLE payload/pagination decision. Next step after the PRs #6-#11 reliability stack.
notes: Implementing firmware pagination (issue #12): Button A short=next page/long=screen toggle, page indicator, MAX_QUOTAS 6->12. Firmware-only PR; companion per-model scraping is separate.
